title: papaver × dubium
intro: Papaver × dubium, also known as the Himalayan poppy or Shirley poppy, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Papaver genus. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as skin irritations and wounds. The plant's alkaloids and flavonoids are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune systems. Herbal preparations of Papaver × dubium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
